matches.moment: Messi's deep link-up drags Egypt's midfield out of shape, creating Argentina's second on the counter; Egypt's reply comes from Salah finally getting isolated 1v1 late on.
Neither goal comes from open play - Embolo heads in a corner's second ball, Diaz equalizes from a free kick that catches a light wall. Set pieces decide this one.

verdict: Today closes the Round of 16 with a day of contrasts rather than a day of formalities. Messi's Argentina remain the most dangerous attacking unit left in the draw by underlying numbers alone - seven goals from 3.82 xG is a player finishing above his chances created, not a mirage - but Egypt's one meaningful data point, a 1-1 draw with a full-strength Belgium, is the single strongest piece of evidence in this report that the Pharaohs won't be brushed aside without a goal of their own, hamstring concern or not. In Vancouver, the day's real storyline is Colombia's defense: one goal conceded in four matches is the best defensive record left in the bracket, but it was built with a 0-0 blank against the only elite side they've faced, and that same profile - stingy but toothless against quality - is exactly what a well-organized Switzerland side, anchored by Xhaka and finishing at a healthier clip than Colombia all tournament, is built to punish just enough to snatch a goal back. Two results, two different shapes: a shootout-adjacent Argentina win built on individual class, and a set-piece-decided stalemate in the Pacific Northwest that neither side's underlying numbers say they deserve to lose. - The Analyst.

players.name: Lionel Messi
players.watch: A second-half burst once Argentina find their attacking rhythm

players.name: Mohamed Salah
players.stat: 1 goal / 1.94 xG this tournament (underperforming, due for correction)
players.watch: Whether he's sprinting at full pace early given his hamstring concern

players.name: James Rodriguez
players.stat: 0 goals / 5 chances created vs DR Congo (most by a Colombian at a WC since 1998)
players.watch: Recovery from flu-like symptoms that forced his half-time substitution vs Ghana

players.name: Granit Xhaka
players.stat: Started and anchored all 4 Swiss matches this tournament (fallback stat)
players.watch: Whether he suppresses James Rodriguez's chance creation as he has all tournament

players.name: Luis Diaz
players.stat: 1 goal this tournament; 5 offsides vs DR Congo (run-timing issue, not pace issue)
players.watch: Whether his run timing corrects against a possibly makeshift Swiss back line
